citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
Some IACUC approved protocols to conduct activities involving animals did not include a rationale for the appropriateness
of numbers of animals used. Proposals #177, #186, #199, and #202 have a rationale for why the use of animals is
necessary but do not have a rationale for the total number of animals to be used. The rationale for the proposed number
of animals to be used enables the IACUC to evaluate the suitability of the number of animals used for the proposal.
Correct by ensuring that IACUC-approved proposals/protocols contain a rationale for the number of animals to be used.
To be corrected by: March 1, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Attending Veterinarian.
Additional Inspectors:
Alexandra Friedman, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.31(e)(2) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
Some IACUC approved protocols to conduct activities involving animals did not include a rationale for the appropriateness 
of numbers of animals used. Proposals #177, #186, #199, and #202 have a rationale for why the use of animals is 
necessary but do not have a rationale for the total number of animals to be used. The rationale for the proposed number 
of animals to be used enables the IACUC to evaluate the suitability of the number of animals used for the proposal. 
Correct by ensuring that IACUC-approved proposals/protocols contain a rationale for the number of animals to be used. 
To be corrected by: March 1, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Attending Veterinarian. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Alexandra Friedman, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
